sections.title: Teaching Experience
content: Teaching Assistant:   ATS772, Aerosol Physics, Clouds, and Climate; Jeffrey R. Pierce
ATS555, Air Pollution; Jeffrey R. Pierce
Lecturer:    ATS772, Aerosol Physics, Clouds, and Climate.
Professional Service
American Association for Aerosol Research CSU chapter – president
Served as the president of the CSU AAAR chapter from 2022-2025.
Micro2Macro Breakout Session Lead
Served as the lead for the Aerosol Chemical and Physical Properties breakout session during the
2024 Micro2Macro workshop.
SOARS protégé – panelist
Served as a panelist for a discussion with SOARS protégé program.
CIRA/ATS Mentoring Program (CAMP) – mentor
Served as a graduate mentor for first year graduate students in the CSU ATS department.

sections.title: Promoting Geoscience Research, Education & Success (PROGRESS) mentoring program – mentor
content: Served as a mentor for program supporting undergraduate women in STEM
CSU Department of Atmospheric Science Code of Conduct – author.
Wrote the initial draft and helped propose a Code of Conduct for the CSU atmospheric science
department.
Little Shop of Physics
Volunteer for the Little Shop of Physics at CSU.
Reviewer – Environmental Science and Technology, Journal of Geophysical Research

sections.title: Publications
content: Submitted:
1. Samuel E. O’Donnell, Xuanlin Du, Jeremy Wakeen, Abraham Dearden, Yicong He, Rebecca J.
Sheesley, James N. Smith, Don R. Collins, Shantanu H. Jathar, Jeffrey R. Pierce: Houston, We
have Particles: Probing The Potential Drivers of New Particle Formation and Growth During
TRACER, submitted to Aerosol Science & Technology, 2025.
2. Jeremy Wakeen, Samuel E. O’Donnell, Don R. Collins, Jeffrey R. Pierce, James N. Smith:
Positive matrix factorization reveals volatility-resolved composition from new particle formation
during α-pinene ozonolysis, submitted to Aerosol Science & Technology, 2026.
Published:
1. Samuel E. O’Donnell, Betty Croft, Bonne Ford, Nicole A. June, Chongai Kuang, Ashish Singh,
Rachel Y.-W. Chang, Don R. Collins, Simo Hakala, Shantanu H. Jathar, Pauli Paasonen, Manish
Shrivastava, James N. Smith, Jeffrey R. Pierce; Going Off Grid: A Comparative Study of the
Lagrangian and Eulerian Perspectives of New Particle Formation Events,
2. Regina Luu, Meredith Schervish, Nicole June, Samuel E. O'Donnell, Shantanu Jathar, Jeffrey
Pierce, Manabu Shiraiwa; Global Simulations of Phase State and Equilibration Timescales of
Secondary Organic Aerosols with GEOS-Chem, ACS Earth and Space Chemistry,
https://doi.org/10.1021/acsearthspacechem.4c00281, 2025.
3. Manish Shrivastava, Jie Zhang, Rahul A. Zaveri, Bin Zhao, Jeffrey R. Pierce, Samuel E.
O’Donnell, Jerome Fast, Brian Gaudet, John Shilling, Alla Zelenyuk, Ben Murphy, Havala Pye,
Qi Zhang, Justin Trousdell, Renyi Zhang, Yixin Li, Qi Chen; Anthropogenic extremely low
volatility organics (ELVOCs) Govern the Growth of Molecular Clusters over the Southern Great
Plains during the Springtime, J. Geophys. Res., http://dx.doi.org/10.1029/2024JD041212, 2024.
4. Samuel E. O'Donnell, Ali Akherati, Yicong He, Anna L. Hodshire, John E. Shilling, Chongai
Kuang, Jerome D. Fast, Fan Mei, Siegfried Schobesberger, Joel A. Thornton, James N. Smith,
Shantanu H. Jathar, Jeffrey R. Pierce; Look up: Probing the vertical profile of new particle
formation and growth in the planetary boundary layer with models and observations, J. Geophys.
5. Samuel E. O’Donnell; Case Study Analysis of Ozone Concentrations in Houston TX During
Hurricane Harvey, UNC Asheville J. Undergrad. Res., 2018, available at:
[https://sites.google.com/a/unca.edu/unc-asheville-journal/2018/search-by-author-
2018?authuser=0]

sections.title: Technical Skills
content: • Python
- Scientific data analysis using numpy, pandas, scipy, xarray.
- Data visualization and plotting using matplotlib and seaborn.
- Basic machine learning with Tensorflow and scikit-learn from machine
learning courses.
• FORTRAN
- Worked with and heavily modified several FORTRAN based code bases.
- Developed an Introduction to FORTRAN document for the research group.
• MATLAB
- Data analysis of new particle formation events and simple aerosol models.
• Shell command line – bash, cmake, slurm.
• High performance computing using CSU’s Asha cluster.
• Atmospheric Modeling:
- GEOS-Chem with TwO-Moment Aerosol Sectional (TOMAS) microphysics package;
- Statistical Oxidation Model (SOM)-TOMAS;
- HYSPLIT.
• Version control using Git and GitHub.
Awards and Honors
2025  2025 Outstanding Reviewer, Geophysical Research Letters.
2025   Wiley, Journal of Geophysical Research: Atmospheres, Top Viewed Article
2023  David L. Dietrich Honorary Scholarship – CSU.
Awarded to a graduate student who has excelled in the study and research of air quality.
2022  Student poster award – American Association for Aerosol Research
2021  First place – American Meteorology Society student poster presentation.
2018   Excellence in Research – Department of Atmospheric Sciences, UNC Asheville
2018   Undergraduate Research Scholar – UNC Asheville

Last saved: April 13, 2026 
Samuel E. O’Donnell, PhD  
Email: Samuel.ODonnell (at) colostate.edu GitHub: samuelod-atmos 
Web: samuelod-atmos.github.io             ORCID: 0000-0003-0977-6000 
Google Scholar: Samuel O’Donnell 
 
Research Interests 
New particle formation; secondary organic aerosol formation; air quality; climate change; 
atmospheric modeling; aerosol-cloud interactions; methane emissions; greenhouse gas emissions.
